name: host-orchestrator
description: Host-side orchestration of the full AFK pipeline — implementation + merge — using Claude Code's native subagent + worktree primitives. Three slash commands (no auto-invocable skills, explicit invocation only): (1) `/parallel-implement-wave` dispatches 2-6 GitHub issues in parallel using `Agent(isolation: "worktree")` (no Docker, no OAuth token gymnastics); (2) `/merge-orchestrate` runs intent-aware serial merge of 2-7 open PRs on host with `merge-resolver` Opus subagent enforcing 5 no-regression criteria; (3) `/afk-pipeline` (v2.1.0) thin per-turn playbook designed to be wrapped by Claude Code's native `/goal` command (v2.1.139+, Haiku verifier between turns) — checks state, picks next productive action (dispatch wave / merge wave / report blocker / declare done), updates PROGRESS.md + state.json, ends turn. Supports `--implement=host|docker` + `--merge=host|docker` flag combinations (default `docker+host`). Recommended entry point: `cc-afk <goal>` bash function (defined in README) that sets `CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW=180000`, `CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_TURNS=50`, `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_THINKING=1`, `API_TIMEOUT_MS=1200000`, then launches `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` with `/goal` wrapping `/afk-pipeline`. Custom Opus subagents: `parallel-implementer` (TDD + vertical-slice + 7 anti-mock anti-patterns + bronze rule self-check) and `merge-resolver` (5 no-regression criteria). Host owns all remote mutations. Audit log append-only. Smart-wave retry. Complementary to sandcastle-max (delegated to for `--implement=docker` and `--merge=docker` modes). Renamed from `merge-orchestrator` v0.1.0 in v2.0.0; v2.1.0 adds `/afk-pipeline` (additive, non-breaking).
